mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 5.5 percent threshold over a 2-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real savings and decreases in the federal funds rate signal abundant liquidity and accommodative policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns in greater than 60% of triggered cases during a walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency falls below 45% when INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3 percent over consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 12-month periods or payroll growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225.0, accelerating inflation forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches the 225.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside accelerating real GDP growth indicate synchronized strength in residential investment and broad economic demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55% of observations during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant payroll growth signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth falls below 0.2 percent.
